title: net_pkt_read return value check may miss partial reads
description:
In lichen_l2.c line 163-168, net_pkt_read() is called to linearize the packet:

    int bytes_read = net_pkt_read(pkt, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len);
    if (bytes_read < 0 || (size_t)bytes_read != pkt_len) {
        LOG_ERR("Failed to linearize packet");
        ...
    }

**VERIFY AGAINST YOUR ZEPHYR VERSION**: The standard Zephyr net_pkt_read() API returns 0 on success and negative errno on error - it does NOT return bytes read. If this is the case:

Trigger: Any TX attempt with non-empty packet.

Bad behavior: net_pkt_read() returns 0 (success), the check '(size_t)bytes_read != pkt_len' evaluates to true (0 != pkt_len), causing TX to fail with 'Failed to linearize packet' even though linearization succeeded.

**However**: If the code compiles and has been tested at all, this would have been caught immediately. Either:
1. The API returns bytes read (different Zephyr version or custom API)
2. net_pkt_read is a different function
3. This code path has never been exercised

**Action**: Check the Zephyr version's net_pkt.h and verify actual behavior. If API returns 0/negative, this is a P0 correctness bug. If API returns bytes read, close this issue.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c lines 163-168
close_reason: Fixed: net_pkt_read returns 0 on success (not bytes read). Removed incorrect partial-read check, now just checks for negative error. Also returns actual error code instead of always -EIO.
